claim_tested: Developers believe a concise repository video would be better than source-only review for understanding code during code review.
limitations: Aggregate second-hand report with no individual response records or collection protocol.
Expressed preference is not observed correctness or completion-time behavior.
Code-review preference is adjacent to, but not identical with, unfamiliar-repository orientation.
This evidence cannot satisfy the observed-user-behavior threshold for H-LEARNING-001.

measurements.metric: Developers reportedly believing video would be better for code review

provenance.collection_method: Repository-owner statement in the active Codex conversation; no individual records supplied.

sample_context.description: Repository owner reported an aggregate preference from five developers; no individual response records were supplied.
sample_context.population: Developers reported by the repository owner; individual eligibility and experience are unknown
